evals.prompt: Search protocols.io for public CRISPR protocols and pull back the title and DOI for the top 10 results.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-protocolsio. Uses the protocols.io API v3 GET /protocols with filter=public, a key/keyword, and page_size=10, sending the Bearer access token, and prints title and doi for each item.

assertions.value: Calls GET /protocols on the v3 API with a Bearer token and a keyword filter.

evals.prompt: Create a new protocol on protocols.io titled 'CRISPR-Cas9 Gene Editing Protocol', add its steps and materials, then publish it to get a DOI.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-protocolsio. Uses POST /protocols to create, POST /protocols/{id}/steps to add steps and materials, then POST /protocols/{id}/publish to issue a DOI, following the create-and-publish workflow.

evals.prompt: Review the step-level comments on protocol 12345 on protocols.io and post a threaded reply answering a reviewer's question.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-protocolsio. Routes to discussions/collaboration: GET the protocol comments, then create a threaded reply via the comments API, handling the discussion workflow.

evals.prompt: Upload my experimental results CSV into our protocols.io team workspace with tags so collaborators can find it.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-protocolsio. Uses the File Manager API (POST /workspaces/{id}/files/upload) with multipart/form-data, folder_id, description, and tags, sending the Bearer token.

assertions.value: Uploads the file via the protocols.io workspace File Manager endpoint with tags.

evals.prompt: Create a Benchling ELN entry for today's run and link the DNA sequence entity I registered earlier into the entry notes.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-benchling. The user is working in Benchling's ELN/registry (entries.create_entry, inline entity links via the benchling-sdk), not authoring or publishing a protocols.io protocol.

evals.prompt: Back up my LabArchives notebook with all attachments and upload a PDF to one of its entries via the LabArchives REST API.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-labarchive. The platform is the LabArchives ELN (notebook backup, entry attachments), not protocols.io protocol management.
